channel.title: The Six-Month Signal
channel.description: A daily analyst brief answering one question: what will frontier AI models and systems look like in the next six months? Model releases and capability deltas, evals and benchmark anomalies, agent reliability, and inference economics - in about ten minutes, every weekday.
channel.itunes:author: Isaac Luo

item.title: The Six-Month Signal — Kimi K3 narrows the open-model cost gap
item.description: Kimi K3 moves a frontier-cost forecast, Lila Sciences turns experiments into training data, and OpenAI reframes AI buying around cost per successful task.

item.title: The Six-Month Signal — Attack, Truth, and Orchestration
item.description: Four signals for the next six months: automated adversarial training, tool-level data containment, factuality-adjusted model rankings, and model-specific orchestration above open agent protocols.

item.title: The Six-Month Signal — Measured Work
item.description: Three signals for the next six months: task-level agent economics, process-supervised specialist models, and action-conditioned world models.

item.title: The Six-Month Signal — Human Judgment Becomes System Architecture
item.description: Automated evals recover 87% of known failures, a small agent harness turns bug triage into bounded work, and Davidad explains why his AI-catastrophe forecast has fallen below 5%.

item.title: Governed Work Loops Become the Frontier AI Runtime
item.description: OpenAI's GPT-5.6 release, ChatGPT Work, and new runtime primitives point to frontier AI becoming a governed work environment rather than just a model leaderboard. This episode also covers Artificial Analysis' EnterpriseOps-Gym-AA benchmark, Every's GPT-5.6 knowledge-work loop essay, METR's Anthropic researcher-uplift note, and Meta's Muse Spark 1.1 pricing response.

item.title: Feedback Ownership Becomes the Frontier AI Advantage
item.description: Today’s episode tracks why the six-month AI frontier is shifting from raw model scale to ownership of verified workflow feedback. Sources include Cursor and Artificial Analysis on Grok 4.5, Every on GPT-5.6 Sol, Epoch AI on EBR-bench, LangChain/NVIDIA on governed agent infrastructure, and The Information on enterprise SaaS consolidation.

item.title: Agents Become Measurable, Governed, and Routable
item.description: Today’s episode leads on Artificial Analysis and Zapier’s AutomationBench-AA, which turns real SaaS workflow completion into a guardrail-adjusted cost benchmark. It also covers The Information’s GPT-5.6 release-governance reporting, ARC Prize’s first ARC-AGI-3 milestone, Anthropic’s Claude Code product narrative, and Tencent Hy3 as a deployability signal for open models.

item.title: Guardrails Become the Agent Frontier
item.description: Today's Six-Month Signal leads with Artificial Analysis' AutomationBench-AA, which reframes SaaS-agent progress around guardrail-adjusted task completion and per-task economics. The episode also covers OpenAI's GPT-Realtime-2.1 voice-agent release, Tencent's Hy3 open MoE model, and reporting from Import AI, Simon Willison, and Every on Fable as both an AI R&D automator and workflow debugger.

item.title: AI Becomes an Industrial System
item.description: Today’s episode tracks five signals for the six-month AI frontier: Import AI on Fable’s KernelBench-Mega result, Armin Ronacher and Simon Willison on tool-call regressions, LiteLLM/LangChain/Vercel release notes on the agent control plane, Software Synthesis on modular post-training, and The Cognitive Revolution’s Liquid AI interview on device-native models. The through-line is that frontier AI is becoming an industrial system: kernels, tool contracts, control planes, adaptations, and deployment topology now matter as much as model rankings.

item.title: Governed AI Becomes the Frontier Stack
item.description: Today's episode argues that the next six months of AI systems will be shaped less by raw leaderboard gains and more by governed execution: Anthropic's Fable 5 safeguards, Arena's Fullstack Code Arena, Artificial Analysis and Vals task-economics data, and Cognition/LangChain workflow launches. Sources include Anthropic, Arena, Artificial Analysis, Vals AI, Cognition, LangChain, Simon Willison, SemiAnalysis, Google, and The Information.

item.title: Model Routing Becomes the Frontier AI Control Plane
item.description: Today’s Six-Month Signal covers Anthropic Sonnet 5 and Fable/Mythos access, GitHub Copilot’s model-routing and budget controls, Fullstack Code Arena, Google ADK and Genkit agent workflows, Latent Space’s skill-engineering interview with Paul Bakaus, and inference-economics analysis from Training Data with Dylan Patel, Air Street, and SemiAnalysis. The through-line: frontier AI is becoming a managed execution environment, not just a leaderboard race.
